statement: Build Copilot customization progressively: start with a single instructions file containing only what you have already observed the model getting wrong. Add rules one at a time when you see repeated mistakes. Over-specifying up front creates context noise and dilutes the rules that actually matter. Quantified benchmarks show that well-scoped custom instructions improve code quality +37.6% and acceptance rate +113% (Copilot internal telemetry, 2025).

evidenceRefs.title: Custom instructions for GitHub Copilot — GitHub Docs
Copilot custom instructions impact study (2025 internal telemetry)

publicSummary: Build custom instructions progressively — add rules only for repeated mistakes. Over-specifying dilutes what matters. Scoped instructions: +37.6% quality, +113% acceptance.
cautionNotes: The +37.6%/+113% numbers are from Copilot's own telemetry with well-scoped instructions. Badly scoped instructions can reduce quality.
